heading: Limitation on actions by and against pipeline carriers
label: 15905 Limitation on actions by and against pipeline carriers
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11706 of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided in , see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1996Pub. L. 104–88section 2 of Pub. L. 104–88section 1301 of this title
amendments: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11706 of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: A pipeline carrier providing transportation or service subject to this part must begin a civil action to recover charges for transportation or service provided by the carrier within 3 years after the claim accrues.

children.heading: .—Overcharges
children.label: b .—Overcharges
children.text: A person must begin a civil action to recover overcharges under section 15904(b)(1) within 3 years after the claim accrues. If an election to file a complaint with the Board is made under section 15904(c)(1), the complaint must be filed within 3 years after the claim accrues.

children.heading: .—Damages
children.label: c .—Damages
children.text: A person must file a complaint with the Board to recover damages under section 15904(b)(2) within 2 years after the claim accrues.

children.heading: .—Extensions
children.label: d .—Extensions
children.text: The limitation periods under subsection (b) are extended for 6 months from the time written notice is given to the claimant by the carrier of disallowance of any part of the claim specified in the notice if a written claim is given to the carrier within those limitation periods. The limitation periods under subsection (b) and the 2-year period under subsection (c) are extended for 90 days from the time the carrier begins a civil action under subsection (a) to recover charges related to the same transportation or service, or collects (without beginning a civil action under that subsection) the charge for that transportation or service if that action is begun or collection is made within the appropriate period.

children.heading: .—Payment
children.label: e .—Payment
children.text: A person must begin a civil action to enforce an order of the Board against a carrier for the payment of money within one year after the date the order required the money to be paid.
payment of the rate for the transportation or service involved,
subsequent refund for overpayment of that rate, or

children.heading: .—Accrual Date
children.label: g .—Accrual Date
children.text: A claim related to a shipment of property accrues under this section on delivery or tender of delivery by the carrier.
